mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override valuation support, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a contraction in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and trigger short-term S&P 500 sell-offs.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during late-cycle phases does not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy outweighs growth expectations, increasing discount rates and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST revisions fail to predict positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.02 over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a rebound in the industrial production index above 100 signals restored manufacturing capacity and easing inflationary pressures, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 100 during post-shock recovery periods do not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with a win rate above 55 percent.
